message: Jalapeño corn dog bites. Hear me out before you dismiss it.
I'm actually really into this - what's the angle you're seeing?
The angle is simple - it's actually technique, not just heat and novelty. Cornmeal crust needs precision or it falls apart. Most places don't care enough to get it right.
The technique angle is exactly what makes it feel different - like, people will taste that it's actually *made* versus just assembled.

The cornmeal ratio is what actually matters here - get that wrong and you're just another fried thing people forget by Friday, but Margaret's nailing something about how a proper crust demands respect.
honestly the cornmeal precision thing - that's what separates this from lazy fried food, and I think that's our whole story right there.
